content:
{
    "improvements": [
        {
            "id": "IMP-001",
            "description": "Define fallback behavior for 'DEGRADED' probe outcomes",
            "rationale": "The probe returns AVAILABLE, UNAVAILABLE, or DEGRADED. The fallback walk algorithm only yields if `result.outcome == AVAILABLE`. If all models in the chain are DEGRADED (e.g., high latency but not down), the chain exhausts and fails the invocation instead of utilizing a degraded model, causing an unnecessary hard outage.",
            "location": "Section 5.3 Fallback walk algorithm",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.95
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-002",
            "description": "Abstract authentication resolution in probe_provider for Bedrock support",
            "rationale": "The SDD assumes `auth: \"{env:OPENAI_API_KEY}\"` for probes. However, Bedrock (mentioned in Assumption-2) requires AWS IAM/SigV4 signing, not a static bearer token. The probe gate will fail for Bedrock models without an auth-resolver abstraction.",
            "location": "Section 4.2.2 Public API (Python canonical)",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.95
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-003",
            "description": "Add lock acquisition timeouts and stale lock recovery for flock",
            "rationale": "The probe cache uses exclusive `flock` on JSON files. If a process crashes (e.g., OOM kill) while holding the lock, subsequent processes will hang indefinitely waiting for the lock, causing a system-wide deadlock for that provider.",
            "location": "Section 4.2.3 flock contract",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.9
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-004",
            "description": "Specify audit event emission contract during LOA_FORCE_LEGACY_MODELS=1",
            "rationale": "If the legacy adapter is triggered via the kill switch, it is unclear if it still emits the new `model.invoke.complete` event. If it doesn't, M1 verification queries will fail or miss data during a rollback, breaking observability precisely when the system is in a degraded state.",
            "location": "Section 8.2 Quarantine vs delete & Section 4.4",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.9
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-005",
            "description": "Implement parallel cache-check/probing for the fallback chain",
            "rationale": "Sequential probing with a 2s timeout means a 3-deep chain where the first two fail adds 4s of latency before the actual LLM invocation begins. Parallel probing of the chain reduces worst-case latency and prevents timeout cascades.",
            "location": "Section 5.3 Fallback walk algorithm",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-006",
            "description": "Split total_latency_ms into probe_latency_ms and invocation_latency_ms",
            "rationale": "Combining probe latency and LLM generation latency in the audit payload obscures actual vendor performance. Separating them is critical for accurate alerting on vendor degradation versus internal probe overhead.",
            "location": "Section 4.4 Audit envelope event",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-007",
            "description": "Define floating-point serialization rules for cross-runtime parity gates",
            "rationale": "Python, Bash (via jq), and TS handle floating-point serialization differently (e.g., trailing zeros on `truncation_coefficient`). Byte-equality tests will spuriously fail across runtimes without strict formatting/normalization rules.",
            "location": "Section 11.2 Cross-runtime parity tests",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.8
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-008",
            "description": "Ensure cross-reference validation applies dynamically to model_aliases_extra",
            "rationale": "The SDD states cross-reference validation happens at config load for `model-config.yaml`. It must explicitly state that dynamically loaded `model_aliases_extra` fallback chains are also validated against the provider registry to prevent runtime ROUTING_MISS errors from operator typos.",
            "location": "Section 3.4 model_aliases_extra extension surface",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-009",
            "description": "Add explicit secret masking to Smoke Fleet GitHub Actions workflow",
            "rationale": "The smoke fleet runs in CI and logs errors. If a provider returns an error containing the API key, or if curl verbose logging is accidentally enabled, secrets will leak in GH Actions logs. Explicit `::add-mask::` steps are required defense-in-depth.",
            "location": "Section 9.1 Workflow",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.9
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-010",
            "description": "Clarify shadow pricing accumulation in audit cost_micro_usd",
            "rationale": "Subscription models don't have a per-call API cost, but AC-4.6 requires shadow pricing. The SDD needs to define whether `cost_micro_usd` in the audit event logs the calculated shadow cost or $0, to ensure the L2 Cost-Budget Enforcer tracks it correctly.",
            "location": "Section 3.3 Per-model required fields & Section 4.4",
            "priority": "LOW",
            "confidence": 0.75
        }
    ],
    "summary": "10 improvements identified, 5 HIGH priority, 4 MEDIUM priority, 1 LOW priority. Focus areas include edge-case handling in the fallback algorithm, concurrency safety for the probe cache, Bedrock authentication, and audit contract consistency during rollbacks."
}
